questions.question: Differentiate between single inheritance and multiple inheritance with examples.
expectedPoints: Single: one derived class from one base class
Multiple: one derived class from multiple base classes
Example of single: Car inherits from Vehicle
Example of multiple: Amphibian inherits from LandAnimal and WaterAnimal

questions.question: Explain the diamond problem in multiple inheritance and how it is resolved using virtual base classes.
expectedPoints: Diamond problem occurs when class inherits from two classes with common base
Results in duplicate copies of base class members
Causes ambiguity in member access
Solved by using virtual inheritance
Virtual inheritance ensures only one copy of base class

questions.question: Describe the order of constructor and destructor execution in multilevel inheritance with an example.
expectedPoints: Constructors execute from base to derived
Destructors execute from derived to base
Example: Class C inherits from B, B inherits from A
Constructor order: A, B, C
Destructor order: C, B, A

questions.question: Compare and contrast the five types of inheritance in C++ with appropriate examples and diagrams.
expectedPoints: Definition and example of single inheritance
Definition and example of multiple inheritance
Definition and example of multilevel inheritance
Definition and example of hierarchical inheritance
Definition and example of hybrid inheritance
Comparison table highlighting differences
Appropriate use cases for each type
Potential issues with each type

questions.question: Design a class hierarchy for a university system using appropriate inheritance types. Justify your design choices.
expectedPoints: Base class: Person with common attributes
Hierarchical inheritance: Student and Faculty derive from Person
Further specialization: UGStudent and PGStudent derive from Student
Multiple inheritance: TeachingAssistant derives from Student and Faculty
Justification of inheritance types used
Consideration of access specifiers
Handling of potential issues like diamond problem
